Package util

Class GridPos

java.lang.Object
util.GridPos
public class GridPos extends Object
The GridPos class represents a 2D coordinate on the game grid.

It provides basic vector-like operations such as addition, direction calculation, and adjacency checks.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    GridPos()
    Constructs a GridPos at (0, 0).
    GridPos(int x, int y)
    Constructs a GridPos with specific coordinates.

    Copy constructor for GridPos.

      Returns:

      Returns:
      The Y coordinate.

      Parameters:
      y - The new Y coordinate.

    • add

      public GridPos add(GridPos other)
      Returns a new GridPos resulting from adding coordinates.
      Parameters:
      other - The coordinate delta to add.
      Returns:
      A new GridPos instance.

    • directionTo

      public Direction directionTo(GridPos other)
      Calculates the cardinal direction from this position to another.
      Parameters:
      other - The target position.
      Returns:
      The Direction to the target, or null if not adjacent.

    • isPosInDirection

      public boolean isPosInDirection(GridPos other, Direction dir)
      Checks if a target position lies in a specific direction relative to this one.
      Parameters:
      other - The position to check.
      dir - The direction to look in.
      Returns:
      true if the target is in the specified direction.

    • add

      public GridPos add(int x, int y)
      Returns a new GridPos resulting from adding raw deltas.
      Parameters:
      x - The X delta.
      y - The Y delta.
      Returns:
      A new GridPos instance.

    • addDirection

      public GridPos addDirection(Direction direction)
      Returns a new GridPos offset by a specific direction.
      Parameters:
      direction - The direction to move in.
      Returns:
      A new GridPos instance.

    • inRange

      public boolean inRange(int xMin, int xMax, int yMin, int yMax)
      Checks if this position falls within a rectangular range.
      Parameters:
      xMin - Minimum X (inclusive).
      xMax - Maximum X (inclusive).
      yMin - Minimum Y (inclusive).
      yMax - Maximum Y (inclusive).
      Returns:
      true if the position is within bounds.
